input_code: const query = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ${req.params.id}`; db.query(query);

vulnerabilities.title: SQL Injection via URL Parameter
vulnerabilities.description: User-supplied id parameter directly interpolated into SQL query without sanitization
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use parameterized queries: db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [req.params.id])

vulnerabilities.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via Template Interpolation
vulnerabilities.description: User input rendered directly without HTML encoding
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use template engine auto-escaping or sanitize: DOMPurify.sanitize(userInput)

vulnerabilities.title: Missing Authorization Check
vulnerabilities.description: Admin verification function exists but is not called before sensitive operations

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded Credential
vulnerabilities.description: API key hardcoded in source code, exposing secrets in version control
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use environment variables: process.env.API_KEY, store in .env file

vulnerabilities.title: CSRF Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: Cross-origin requests include credentials without CSRF token validation
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add CSRF token: fetch(url, { headers: { 'X-CSRF-Token': csrfToken } })

vulnerabilities.title: Weak Session Secret and Insecure Cookie
vulnerabilities.description: Short secret for session encryption and secure flag disabled on cookies
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use crypto.randomBytes(32), set cookie: { secure: true, httpOnly: true }

vulnerabilities.title: Stored XSS via Image Error Handler
vulnerabilities.description: Malicious payload stored and executed when image fails to load
vulnerabilities.remediation: Sanitize all user input: import DOMPurify from 'dompurify';

vulnerabilities.title: Hardcoded Passwords in Source Code
vulnerabilities.description: Credentials stored in plaintext within application code
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use password hashing: bcrypt.hashpw(), verify: bcrypt.checkpw(), store in secure vault

input_code: def login(username, password): return db.query(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='{username}'")

vulnerabilities.title: SQL Injection in Login Function
vulnerabilities.description: User input directly concatenated into SQL query enabling authentication bypass
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE username=%s', (username,))

vulnerabilities.title: Code Injection via eval()
vulnerabilities.description: User-controlled input passed to eval() allows arbitrary code execution
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove eval(), use sandboxed execution: new Function() with strict input validation or VM2 module
